channel.title: CyberNetSec - Latest Cybersecurity Articles
channel.description: Latest cybersecurity threat intelligence articles from the past 3 days

item.title: cPanel Zero-Day Auth Bypass (CVE-2026-41940) Actively Exploited for Months Before Patch
item.description:
A critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-41940, in the widely used cPanel & WHM web hosting platform was actively exploited as a zero-day for at least two months before a patch was released. The flaw, which has a CVSS score of 9.8, allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to gain fu...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Cyberattack, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: QR Code Phishing Surges 146% in Q1 2026, Microsoft Warns
item.description:
According to Microsoft's Q1 2026 threat intelligence report, QR code-based phishing (quishing) has become the fastest-growing email attack vector, with volumes jumping 146% between January and March. Attackers are increasingly hiding malicious QR codes within PDF attachments to bypass email security...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: UK Cyber Survey: 43% of Businesses Breached, But Only 25% Have an IR Plan
item.description:
The UK government's latest Cyber Security Breaches Survey for 2025/2026 reveals a landscape of persistent risk, with 43% of businesses (an estimated 612,000) experiencing a breach in the past year. Phishing remains the dominant attack vector, implicated in 85% of successful breaches. Despite the hig...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: MOVEit Automation Hit with Critical 9.8 CVSS Auth Bypass Flaw (CVE-2026-4670)
item.description:
Progress Software has issued an urgent security alert for a critical authentication bypass vulnerability, CVE-2026-4670, in its MOVEit Automation software. The flaw, which has been assigned a CVSS score of 9.8, could allow an unauthenticated attacker to gain unauthorized access and potentially admin...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Palo Alto Networks to Acquire Portkey, an AI Gateway Startup, to Secure Autonomous AI Agents
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks has announced its planned acquisition of Portkey, a startup specializing in AI Gateway technology. The move is a strategic effort to address the emerging security challenges posed by the rise of autonomous AI agents in the enterprise. Portkey provides a centralized control plane t...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Security Operations, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: That AI Extension Helping You Write? It's Actually a RAT Stealing Your Data
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 has discovered at least 18 malicious browser extensions masquerading as Generative AI productivity tools. These extensions, some downloaded by thousands, are not enhancing productivity but are instead functioning as Remote Access Trojans (RATs), infostealers, and search h...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Threat Intelligence, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical 'Copy Fail' Linux Flaw (CVE-2026-31431) Gives Instant Root on Major Distros
item.description:
A critical logic flaw in the Linux kernel, dubbed 'Copy Fail' and tracked as CVE-2026-31431, has been disclosed, affecting nearly all major distributions since 2017. Discovered by Theori, the vulnerability allows any unprivileged local user to gain full root privileges with a simple, highly reliable...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical GitHub RCE Flaw (CVE-2026-3854) Allowed Full Server Compromise via Single 'git push'
item.description:
A critical remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability in GitHub, tracked as CVE-2026-3854, has been disclosed by security firm Wiz. The flaw allowed any authenticated user with push access to a repository to execute arbitrary commands on GitHub's backend servers using a specially crafted `git push` c...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Supply Chain Attack, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Medtronic Confirms Data Breach After ShinyHunters Claims Theft of 9 Million Records and Corporate Data
item.description:
Medical device manufacturer Medtronic has confirmed it sustained a cybersecurity incident following claims by the ShinyHunters threat group. The group alleged it stole over 9 million records and terabytes of corporate data, issuing a 'pay or leak' ultimatum. Medtronic stated the breach was contained...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: CISA and Partners Release Joint Guidance for Applying Zero Trust Principles to OT Environments
item.description:
The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), in partnership with the Department of War, DOE, FBI, and DOS, has published a comprehensive guide for implementing Zero Trust security principles within Operational Technology (OT) environments. The guidance provides a roadmap for sec...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Industrial Control Systems, Regulatory

Read Full Article →

item.title: Europol IOCTA Report: AI, Encryption, and Data Theft are Fueling an Industrialized Cybercrime Wave
item.description:
Europol's 2026 Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) report warns of an industrialized cybercrime landscape where AI, encryption, and proxies are making attacks faster and more sophisticated. The report highlights a significant shift in ransomware tactics towards data theft and extortio...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: ShinyHunters Leaks 1.4 Million Udemy User Records, Including Financial Data, After Failed Extortion
item.description:
The ShinyHunters cybercrime group has publicly leaked a database containing 1.4 million records from the online learning platform Udemy after a 'pay or leak' extortion attempt failed. The compromised data is extensive, including user and instructor PII, contact information, and sensitive financial p...

Severity: High

Categories: Data Breach, Threat Actor, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: New Stealthy Python Backdoor 'DEEP#DOOR' Steals Credentials Using Legitimate Tunneling Service
item.description:
Security researchers from Securonix have discovered a sophisticated, multi-stage Python backdoor named DEEP#DOOR. The malware operates as a full-featured Remote Access Trojan (RAT), beginning with an obfuscated batch script that embeds the Python payload. It disables Windows security controls, estab...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Threat Intelligence, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: VECT 2.0 Ransomware Flaw Means Paying the Ransom is Pointless—Large Files are Wiped Forever
item.description:
Security researchers at Check Point have uncovered a critical design flaw in the VECT 2.0 ransomware that causes it to permanently destroy files larger than 128 KB instead of encrypting them. This bug, present in the Windows, Linux, and ESXi variants, discards essential decryption keys (nonces) duri...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Malware, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: ShinyHunters Ransomware Spree: Carnival, Zara's Parent, and 40+ Firms Breached in Massive Campaign
item.description:
The ShinyHunters ransomware group has claimed responsibility for a large-scale attack campaign that has compromised more than 40 organizations worldwide. The victims, listed on the group's data leak site, span the retail, insurance, and hospitality sectors. High-profile targets include Carnival Corp...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Europol Warns of AI-Powered 'Industrialized Cybercrime' in IOCTA 2026 Report
item.description:
Europol's 2026 Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) highlights a major shift towards the 'industrialization' of cybercrime, significantly accelerated by the use of Artificial Intelligence. The report states that AI is being used to increase the scale, speed, and efficiency of attacks. ...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: MITRE Warns of New Cyber Risks in AI and Cloud-Connected Medical Devices
item.description:
A new discussion paper from MITRE warns that the rapid integration of emerging technologies like AI, cloud computing, and post-quantum cryptography into medical devices is creating new and complex cybersecurity risks that could directly impact patient safety. The report, "Cybersecurity Risk Analysis...

Severity: Informational

Categories: IoT Security, Policy and Compliance, Cloud Security

Read Full Article →

item.title: Ransomware Turf War: 0APT and KryBit Groups Hack Each Other in Public Feud, Leaking Ops Data
item.description:
A rare public conflict has erupted between two rival ransomware groups, 0APT and KryBit. The feud, which played out between March 28 and April 12, 2026, involved both groups hacking each other and leaking sensitive operational data. 0APT initiated the spat by leaking KryBit's admin panel. KryBit ret...

Severity: Low

Categories: Threat Actor, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: China-Based Silver Fox APT Expands Espionage Campaign Across Asia with Fake Tax Audits
item.description:
The China-based threat group Silver Fox has launched a new wave of attacks targeting businesses and individuals across Asia. The group, which has been active since at least 2022, uses fake tax audit notifications and counterfeit software update alerts to deliver malware. According to analysis by S2W...

Severity: High

Categories: Threat Actor, Cyberattack, Phishing

Read Full Article →

item.title: Entertainment Industry Failing on Security Basics, MPA Report Reveals Widespread Risks
item.description:
The Motion Picture Association's (MPA) Trusted Partner Network (TPN) has released its first TPN STAR Report, revealing systemic security vulnerabilities across the global entertainment supply chain. The report finds that while most organizations have security policies, inconsistent execution of basi...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Policy and Compliance, Data Breach

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical Unpatched RCE Flaw in Hugging Face's LeRobot AI Platform Puts Robotics Systems at Risk
item.description:
A critical, unpatched remote code execution (RCE) vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-25874 with a CVSS score of 9.3, has been discovered in Hugging Face's popular open-source robotics platform, LeRobot. The flaw stems from an insecure data deserialization practice using `pickle.loads()` over unauthe...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Supply Chain Attack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Singapore Plans Major Cybersecurity Overhaul, Mandating PQC and Expanding CII Oversight
item.description:
Singapore is set to significantly strengthen its national cybersecurity posture with a series of regulatory updates announced by the government. The changes, to be rolled out over the next two years, will expand cybersecurity obligations to systems connected to Critical Information Infrastructure (C...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: AI-Driven Attacks Fueling MSP Supply Chain Risk, Guardz Report Finds
item.description:
A new report from cybersecurity firm Guardz reveals a grim reality for SMBs, with nine out of ten having compromised users, largely due to AI-accelerated attacks. The 2026 State of MSP Threat Report highlights a massive surge in losses from Business Email Compromise (BEC) and identifies the abuse of...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Threat Intelligence, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: Checkmarx Details Supply Chain Attack Stemming from Trivy Scanner Vulnerability
item.description:
Application security firm Checkmarx has provided a detailed timeline of a supply chain attack that began with the exploitation of a vulnerability in the Trivy scanner. The attack, which started on March 23, 2026, led to unauthorized access to Checkmarx's GitHub environment. Attackers published malic...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Data Breach, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Wisconsin Hospital Mile Bluff Medical Center Hit by Ransomware, Enters Downtime Procedures
item.description:
Mile Bluff Medical Center in Mauston, Wisconsin, has confirmed it is recovering from a ransomware attack that occurred in April 2026. The attack encrypted files on the medical center's network, causing temporary disruptions to computer and phone systems. Clinical teams were forced to revert to estab...

Severity: High

Categories: Ransomware, Data Breach, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Foxit PDF Reader Flaw (CVE-2026-5942) Could Lead to Information Disclosure
item.description:
A use-after-free vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-5942, has been disclosed in affected versions of Foxit PDF Reader. The flaw, which requires a user to open a malicious file, can be exploited to disclose sensitive information from the system's memory. While rated with a CVSS score of 3.3, security...

Severity: Medium

Categories: Vulnerability, Patch Management

Read Full Article →
